annotation_target.structure: Cantabile second theme — yearning melody arch over lush accompaniment
key_moments: m17 melody reaches toward upper register
teaching_goals: singing cantabile line — RH sings like a cello
LH accompaniment is lush but always under the melody
style_tips: mp rising to mf — this phrase needs dynamic shape
rubato appropriate — feel the phrase's weight
pedal is essential here for legato

teaching_notes.note: This is the heart of the piece — every note should feel inevitable.

target_trace.objective: Read mm. 15–18 of Clair de Lune (Suite bergamasque, III), view the piano roll, analyze the phrase, play in a loop, and predict the continuation.

session.content: Show me measures 15–18 of Clair de Lune — this is where the famous melody enters. Describe it.
Let me view the piano roll for mm. 15–18.

Measures 15–18 bring the famous Clair de Lune cantabile melody — the piece's emotional center. A singing line emerges in the right hand over a lush broken-chord accompaniment. The melody has a yearning quality, arching upward. Let me loop mm. 15–18.
Clair de Lune mm. 15–18: famous cantabile melody enters, yearning arch, lush accompaniment. Teaching focus: singing touch, phrase shaping.
